primary_reason: This review compares tick-borne disease surveillance approaches and identifies standardization, reporting, and integration problems.
This single equine case documents etiologic diagnosis without a transferable epidemiological contribution.
This study summarizes routinely submitted rabies specimens and their geographic distribution without developing or evaluating a reusable surveillance adjustment or measurement method.
This systematic review applies risk-of-bias assessment, meta-analysis, and GRADE to equine herpesvirus vaccines.
This review concerns allergic reactions to serum eye drops and supplies no concrete route for transferring its analysis to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This review discusses viral associations and biological pathogenesis of glaucoma without an eligible epidemiological method.
This intervention evaluation uses reporting performance indicators to assess pharmacovigilance training and mentoring.
This paper develops an immunity-aware epidemic model and physics-informed neural-network procedure for parameter inference.
This applied comparison evaluates mechanistic dengue forecasting models incorporating climate and underreporting uncertainty.
This study compares three rabies-exposure reporting sources to diagnose surveillance undercoverage and documentation gaps.
